content: SE treatment follows time-based algorithm. 0-5 minutes: stabilize ABCs, secure IV access, check glucose (give thiamine 100mg + D50 if hypoglycemic), obtain labs. 5-20 minutes (first-line): benzodiazepine—lorazepam 0.1mg/kg IV (max 4mg/dose, may repeat once) OR midazolam 10mg IM (preferred if no IV access) OR diazepam 0.15mg/kg IV. 20-40 minutes (second-line): fosphenytoin 20mg PE/kg IV (max 150mg/min) OR valproic acid 40mg/kg IV (max 6mg/kg/min, avoid in liver disease/pregnancy) OR levetiracetam 60mg/kg IV (max 4500mg, favorable safety profile) OR phenobarbital 15mg/kg IV. 40-60 minutes (third-line/refractory): anesthetic infusion requiring intubation and continuous EEG—midazolam infusion (0.2mg/kg bolus then 0.05-2mg/kg/hr), propofol (1-2mg/kg bolus then 30-200mcg/kg/min, watch for propofol infusion syndrome), or pentobarbital (5-15mg/kg bolus then 0.5-5mg/kg/hr). Goal: burst-suppression on EEG for 24-48 hours. Super-refractory SE: consider immunotherapy (steroids, IVIG, PLEX), ketogenic diet, hypothermia, multiple drug combinations, epilepsy surgery evaluation.
